threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you still look puzzled at the 'no cycling' signs near the park while everyone else seems to know the rules by heart.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Croydon last weekend, and a guy was trying to barter a pint of lager for a phone charger. The driver didn't bat an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It hit me when the bakery lady started remembering my regular order, and the kids in the playground waved while I walked by. That's home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a graffiti artist working live on the U-Bahn just moments before a train rolls in. It's like art meets transit chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they can name the exact month the taqueria on 24th changed their salsa recipe—and the year they went cashless.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the bus this morning. Turns out he was headed to a wedding. Only in the Tenderloin do you get black tie in the daytime.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think La Rambla is the heart of the city, but they miss the real vibe in Gràcia’s plazas, where locals gather and life feels genuine.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot a recent arrival by their bewilderment staring at the T-10 ticket machines, utterly baffled by the options.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You don’t know the way to navigate around a roundabout without looking lost—every local has a secret route they've mastered.
Caught a guy on the 36 last night singing opera at the back. Had the whole bus swinging in their seats, weirdly uplifting for a Tuesday.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a busker playing the kora on Malchower Straße while a crowd gathered, I knew this place had a pulse unlike anywhere else.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do they get in-depth discussions about the city’s bus routes over a beer at the corner kiosk—true local tradition.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission when they can name more than two taco spots on 24th without missing a beat. The newbies stick to the tourist traps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy juggling on the 19 Polk today while the bus came to a full stop. Just your typical Tenderloin commute, folks.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals hit the beach every weekend, but many of us prefer a quiet afternoon in the Plaça del Sol with a book and a vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still struggling to find a café that serves a proper cortado and asking for ‘milk coffee’ at 7pm.
